understanding.goal_understanding: Reach the goal gem tile marked `+` on each level without landing on spike tiles (`u`/`v`), completing levels within the per-attempt turn limit (50 turns) and limited attempts (2 per level), then continue through subsequent levels until the game is won.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn you choose a horizontal move (`MOVE_LEFT`/`MOVE_RIGHT`) or a `CLICK` on a specific breakable/clickable tile (e.g., `x`) to remove it. After any horizontal move, gravity automatically carries the player in the current gravity direction until they land on a solid/support tile (`o`/`m`/`w`), reach the goal `+`, or hit a hazard/spike which immediately ends the run. `CLICK` changes the map (removing targeted breakables) without moving the player and is used to open a fall path in the gravity direction; `UNDO` reverts the previous action. Planning is guided by the state’s landing previews (`left_landing`/`right_landing`) and corridor scan to find safe passage columns that drop to a new y-level/section.

understanding.goal_understanding: Reach the goal gem tile marked `+` on each level without landing on spike tiles (`u`/`v`), completing levels within the per-attempt turn limit (50 turns) and limited attempts (2 per level), then continue through subsequent levels until the game is won.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn you choose a horizontal move (`MOVE_LEFT`/`MOVE_RIGHT`) or a `CLICK` on a specific breakable/clickable tile (e.g., `x`) to remove it. After any horizontal move, gravity automatically carries the player in the current gravity direction until they land on a solid/support tile (`o`/`m`/`w`), reach the goal `+`, or hit a hazard/spike which immediately ends the run. `CLICK` changes the map (removing targeted breakables) without moving the player and is used to open a fall path in the gravity direction; `UNDO` reverts the previous action. Planning is guided by the state’s landing previews (`left_landing`/`right_landing`) and corridor scan to find safe passage columns that drop to a new y-level/section.
